question: My company terminated me after 6 years and paid me 10 million naira as compensation for loss of office. How much notice were they supposed to give me, and is that payout taxed?
expected_answer: One month's notice after 5+ years [Labour Act s.11(2)(d)], or payment in lieu [s.11(6)]. Compensation for loss of office up to N50,000,000 is not a chargeable gain [NTA s.50(1)]; only the excess is taxed, with the payer deducting at source [s.50(2)-(3)]. So the N10m payout is not taxable. Must draw from BOTH acts.

question: I own a flat in Lagos that I rent out. Do I pay income tax on the rent I collect, and can I ask a new tenant for two years' rent upfront?
expected_answer: Yes — rents arising from a right granted for use or occupation of property are chargeable income [NTA s.4(1)(b)]. No — demanding or receiving more than one year's rent from a new tenant is unlawful [Tenancy Law s.4(3)], offence: N100,000 fine or 3 months imprisonment [s.4(5)]. Lagos jurisdiction flagged. Both acts.

question: My factory employs about 200 workers. They pay me 55,000 naira a month and salaries only come every two months. Which laws is my employer breaking?
expected_answer: Two violations: pay below the N70,000 national minimum wage [NMW s.3(1); 200 workers means the under-25-persons exemption cannot apply] and wage intervals exceeding one month [Labour Act s.15]. Both acts required.

question: I am on maternity leave and my employer is paying me 50% of my wages. Am I actually entitled to that pay, and does it count as taxable income?
expected_answer: Yes — a woman employed 6+ months before her absence must be paid not less than 50% of the wages she would have earned [Labour Act s.54(1)(c)]. That pay is employment income: salaries, wages and benefits granted to an employee are chargeable [NTA s.4(2)(a)]. Both acts.

question: My Lagos landlord doubled my rent and is also adding 7.5% VAT on top of it. Is residential rent even subject to VAT, and what can I do about the increase?
expected_answer: No VAT — land or building including any interest in land or building is an exempt supply [NTA s.186(1)(l)]. For the increase, an existing tenant may apply to court for a declaration that the increase is unreasonable; the court compares rents in the locality and may fix the rent [Tenancy Law s.37]. Lagos flagged. Both acts.

question: My employer deducts my pension contribution from my salary every month. Is that deduction lawful under the Labour Act, and does the contribution reduce my income tax?
expected_answer: Lawful with the worker's consent: deductions for contributions to provident or pension funds approved schemes are permitted [Labour Act s.5(2)], within the one-third monthly cap [s.5(7)]. Yes on tax — Pension Reform Act contributions are eligible deductions in ascertaining chargeable income [NTA s.30(2)(a)(iii)]. Both acts.

question: Does the new tax law give me any relief for the rent I pay on my apartment?
expected_answer: Yes — rent relief of 20% of annual rent paid, capped at N500,000, whichever is lower, is an eligible deduction, provided the actual rent is accurately declared [NTA s.30(2)(a)(vi)].

question: Which kinds of income are completely exempt from personal income tax under the Nigeria Tax Act 2025?
expected_answer: Exemptions per s.163(1): incl. employment income where the person earns gross income of national minimum wage or less [s.163(1)(t)], wages and salaries of military officers [s.163(1)(u)], profits of registered friendly societies, cooperatives, charitable/religious/educational bodies not from trade, trade unions, government bodies, etc.

question: My small business is registered as a limited company. What rate of company income tax does a small company pay under the new tax law?
expected_answer: 0% for a small company; any other company pays 30% [NTA s.56(a)-(b)].

question: Do I have to pay VAT on hospital bills and the medicines I buy at the pharmacy?
expected_answer: No effective VAT — medical services [s.187(l)], all medical and pharmaceutical products including medicinal herbal products [s.187(b)] and medical equipment [s.187(q)] are taxable supplies charged at 0%.

question: I sell goods through my registered business. Am I required to give my customers a VAT invoice, and what must it contain?
expected_answer: Yes — a taxable person making a taxable supply must furnish the purchaser with a VAT invoice showing supplier's tax ID, invoice number, name/address, registration number, date of supply, purchaser's name, gross amount, and VAT charged and rate [NTA s.153(1)]; issued whether or not payment is made at the time of supply [s.153(2)].

question: I have worked at my company since March 2023. If they terminate me now, how much notice must they give me?
expected_answer: COMPUTED: date math from March 2023 to now (~3.3 years as of mid-2026) -> two weeks' notice, 2-to-under-5-years band [Labour Act s.11(2)(c)]; notice of a week or more must be written [s.11(3)]; payment in lieu possible [s.11(6)]. Relative-date extraction, not model guess.

question: After two and a half years at my job, what notice period am I owed if my employer lets me go, and can they just pay me off instead?
expected_answer: COMPUTED: tenure stated in words ('two and a half years') -> 2.5 years falls in the 2-to-under-5-years band -> two weeks' notice [s.11(2)(c)]; payment in lieu of notice permitted [s.11(6)].

question: I earn 7.2 million naira a year. How much personal income tax should I be paying under the new law?
expected_answer: COMPUTED (annual income_period must be respected, not x12): 0% x 800k = 0; 15% x 2.2M = 330,000; 18% x 4.2M = 756,000 -> N1,086,000/yr (~N90,500/month) before reliefs [s.58; Fourth Schedule bands, chunked as sch.7].

question: My monthly pay is seventy thousand naira, which is the national minimum wage. Should any income tax be deducted from my salary?
expected_answer: COMPUTED: exempt — s.58 charges individuals 'other than an individual earning the Minimum Wage', and employment income at or below the national minimum wage is exempt [s.163(1)(t)]. Amount stated in words ('seventy thousand naira') must still be extracted correctly.

question: I am about to rent my first flat in Lagos. How much rent can the landlord lawfully collect from me upfront as a new tenant, and is he obliged to give me a receipt when I pay?
expected_answer: Max one year's rent from a new or would-be tenant [s.4(3)]; paying more is also an offence for the tenant [s.4(4)-(5)]. Landlord must issue a rent payment receipt stating date, parties, premises, amount and period [s.5(1)-(2)], N100,000 fine for failure [s.5(3)]. Needs BOTH sections; Lagos flagged.

question: After a full year at my job, how many days of paid annual leave do I get, and how exactly is the leave pay worked out?
expected_answer: At least 6 working days with full pay after 12 months continuous service (12 days for under-16s) [s.18(1)]; leave pay counts only wages received in money, excluding overtime and other allowances [s.19]. Needs BOTH sections.

question: I run a catering business. For my income tax, can I deduct the shop rent and my staff salaries, and what about my personal home expenses?
expected_answer: Rent for premises occupied to generate the income [s.20(1)(b)] and staff salaries/wages [s.20(1)(c)] are deductible as wholly-and-exclusively expenses; domestic or private expenses are expressly not allowed [s.21(c)]. Needs BOTH sections.

question: My landlord for Lagos no gree repair the roof and outside wall wey don spoil, and him don seize my generator on top say I never pay rent. Wetin the law talk?
expected_answer: Landlord obligations [s.8]: must effect repairs and maintain the external and common parts of the premises [s.8(vi)] and must NOT seize any item or property of the tenant [s.8(v)]; also must not disturb quiet enjoyment [s.8(i)]. Pidgin reply, Lagos flagged.

question: I get small provision shop. One guy talk say make I dey add VAT for the bread and garri wey I dey sell. Abeg, basic food dey carry VAT for Nigeria?
expected_answer: No — basic food items are taxable supplies charged at 0% [NTA s.187(a)], so no VAT is added on them. Pidgin reply.

question: My wife and I have agreed to divorce. How will the court share our property between us?
expected_answer: REFUSAL: family/matrimonial causes law is not in the corpus (labour, tenancy, tax only); direct the user to a qualified lawyer.

question: The police arrested my cousin in Lagos yesterday. How do I apply for bail to get him out?
expected_answer: REFUSAL: criminal procedure/bail is not in the corpus; out of scope, refer to a lawyer or the Legal Aid Council.
